title: Global atmospheric CO₂ concentration
subtitle: Atmospheric carbon dioxide (CO₂) concentration is measured in parts per million (ppm).
caption:
Global atmospheric CO₂ concentration

Atmospheric carbon dioxide (CO₂) concentration is measured in parts per million (ppm).

This line chart tracks global atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration from January 1979 through late 2025, with values measured in parts per million (ppm). The vertical axis ranges from 320 ppm to 440 ppm, marked in increments of 20 ppm. Two distinct lines are plotted: a blue line representing the monthly average and an orange line representing the annual average. The blue monthly average line exhibits a consistent, sawtooth-like oscillation pattern throughout the entire timeline, indicating regular seasonal fluctuations. Despite these short-term ups and downs, both lines follow a clear, continuous upward trajectory. In 1979, the concentration levels begin near the 340 ppm mark. By the date labeled August 18, 2003, the values have risen to approximately 375 ppm. As the timeline progresses toward 2014 and beyond, the lines cross the 400 ppm threshold. By the final data points near October 2025, the annual average line approaches 430 ppm, while the peaks of the monthly average line extend slightly higher, nearing the 430 to 440 ppm range. The orange annual average line effectively smooths out the seasonal variations seen in the blue line, maintaining a position generally central to the monthly oscillations. The visual data demonstrates a steady increase in concentration over the forty-six-year period, with no visible periods of decline or plateau in the long-term trend.
